todaysEvents.summary: Iran IRGC commander Ali Abdollahi threatens to block Gulf, Red Sea, and Gulf of Oman shipping if US naval blockade persists; signals potential Houthi reactivation (NBC, Fortune, Newsweek, Apr 16).
todaysEvents.delta: Escalation: Iran widens threat from Hormuz to full regional shipping disruption

todaysEvents.summary: Pakistan Field Marshal Asim Munir arrives in Tehran with US message; Iranian FM Araghchi meets Pakistani army chief - most direct diplomatic contact since blockade imposed; WH: 'we feel good about prospects of a deal'; Trump: war 'very close to over' (CNN, NPR, Apr 16).
todaysEvents.delta: De-escalation: Pakistan mediates actively; WH optimism strongest signal yet

todaysEvents.summary: Brent $94.60 confirmed (TradingEconomics Apr 16, -0.3%); WTI $90.79 confirmed (-0.5%); US Treasury Bessent threatens secondary sanctions on Iran oil buyers targeting Chinese banks; Israel-Lebanon ceasefire talks begin (Netanyahu security cabinet, Apr 16).
todaysEvents.delta: Markets flat on offsetting signals; secondary sanctions tighten enforcement

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Second round of US-Iran Islamabad talks resumes within days; blockade serves as negotiating leverage rather than trigger for direct confrontation. Framework agreement on nuclear commitment + partial Hormuz sovereignty emerges before Apr 21 ceasefire expiry. P&I restoration phased over 2-3 weeks; Brent retraces toward $85-90/bbl as risk premium unwinds; iTraxx Asia IG tightens below 165bp. Netanyahu-Lebanon track provides parallel de-escalation channel.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: US blockade and Iran-bound port closure persist for 2-4 weeks; commercial Hormuz transit for non-Iranian flows continues but with elevated insurance costs. Second round of talks produces incremental progress but no framework before Apr 21 ceasefire expiry. Iran oil waiver expiration tightens sanctions enforcement. Brent stabilises $90-105/bbl; JKM $19-22/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG 170-195bp. IRGC conducts asymmetric harassment without triggering full kinetic engagement.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Second round of talks collapses; ceasefire lapses Apr 21 without extension. IRGC executes Gulf-wide shipping disruption - blocks Red Sea via Houthi reactivation, Gulf of Oman, and Persian Gulf. Full ceasefire collapse; resumed US-Israel air campaign. Kharg Island seizure or Saudi Aramco attack brings GCC production under acute threat. Brent spikes $130-175/bbl; LNG markets seize; ASEAN recession risk becomes acute. Gold surges past $5,000/oz.
